questions.question: What output would the following query produce – SELECT "Res""ult"?
What output would the following query produce – SELECT 8*8 FROM Customers?
options: D. 64 repeated for every record in the customer table

questions.question: What output would the following query produce – SELECT * FROM Customers ORDER\nBY field1 ASC, field1 DESC?
options: A. All columns in the Customers table order by field1 descending (Z-A)
B. All columns in the Customers table order by field1 ascending (A-Z)
C. All columns in the Customers table with no order applied

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a DBMS?
LIMIT is the ANSI standard way to restrict the number of rows returned in a query?
“SELECT field1, MAX(field2) FROM Customers” will return _______.
options: A. the maximum value in field2 and the field1 that corresponds to it
B. the maximum value in field2 and a potentially unrelated field1
C. the maximum value in field2 and the last field1 in the table
D. the maximum value in field2 and the first field1 in the table

questions.question: To gather all of rows where field1 is either ‘A’, ‘B’, or ‘C’ the WHERE clause would be\n“WHERE _______”.
options: C. field1 = 'A' and field1 = 'B' and field1 = 'C'

questions.question: _______ are all DML statements.
options: B. SELECT, WHERE, ORDER BY, and INTO

questions.question: The UNION operator is used to _______.
options: A. combine the result sets of two or more SELECT statements
B. uniquely identify the rows in a table
C. combine two disparate tables into one
D. extract the columns from one table to alter the columns from a second table

questions.question: “_______” would add a new field to the Customers table with the name email.
options: A. MODIFY TABLE Customers ADD COLUMN Email varchar(255)
B. ALTER TABLE Customers NEW COLUMN Email varchar(255)
C. ALTER TABLE Customers ADD Email varchar(255)
D. MODIFY TABLE Customers NEW Email varchar(255)

questions.question: Prepared SQL code that can be stored and called over and over is call a stored _______.
Which of the following is NOT an SQL logical operator?
Given the table mytable, where field1 is either 1 or 0 and field2 is either 1 or 0, what will\nbe the result of the query “SELECT field1|field2 FROM mytable”?
options: A. All rows from mytable with field1 and field2 concatenated together
B. Only rows from mytable where either field1 or field2 is 1
C. Field1 if its 1, else field2 if its 1, otherwise no row
D. A 0 for each row where field1 and field2 are 0; all other rows will be 1

questions.question: A GROUP BY clause cannot contain aggregate functions?
_______ are used to restrict the type of data that can go into a table.
What of the following is NOT a true statement about the differences between primary and\ncandidate keys?
options: A. A primary key cannot be null, but a candidate key can
B. A primary key is a candidate key
C. A table can have multiple primary or candidate keys
D. A candidate key may not be a primary key

questions.question: Assuming the maximum value for the varchar datatype is 255, why would it be necessary\nto use any value other than 255 in the definition?
options: A. Varchar(50) uses much less space than varchar(255)
B. You don’t think they’d use it so you wouldn’t assign the extra space
C. The business logic has expectations for the field size
D. It wouldn’t be necessary – standards say use 255

questions.question: The process of adding a unique value to the beginning or the end of a password before\nthe password is hashed is called _______.
When using a UNION in a SELECT statement _______.
options: A. the two queries on either side of the UNION must be from the same table
B. the two queries on either side of the UNION must not be from the same table
C. the results are sorted ascending by default
D. the results are distinct by default

questions.question: _______ is a data structure technique which allows you to quickly retrieve records from a\ndatabase file.
Which of the following is NOT a command used to control transactions?
The acronym _______ representing the standard properties of a transaction?
Which of the answers below is categorized as a data manipulation language command?
The SQL statement “_______”, will remove all records from a table and will lock each\nrow prior to the delete.

How many rows would be returned from the following query?
SELECT f_name, l_name, descrip, paid
FROM member a
INNER JOIN member_item b on a.m_id = b.m_id
INNER JOIN item c on b.item_id = c.id
WHERE paid BETWEEN 33.50 and 90;
Which query will get a unique list of the items Jan Smith purchased?
options:
A. SELECT DISTINCT descrip
FROM member a
INNER JOIN member_item b ON a.m_id = b.m_id
INNER JOIN Item c ON b.item_id = c.id
WHERE m_id = 1117

B. SELECT DISTINCT (f_name, descrip)
FROM member
INNER JOIN member_item a ON a.m_id = member.m_id
INNER JOIN item ON a.item_id = item.id
WHERE f_name = "Jan"

C. SELECT DISTINCT descrip
FROM member_item a
INNER JOIN member b ON a.m_id = b.m_id
INNER JOIN item c ON a.item_id = c.id
WHERE c.m_id = 1117

D. SELECT DISTINCT descrip
FROM member_item a
INNER JOIN member b ON a.m_id = b.m_id
INNER JOIN item c ON a.item_id = c.id
WHERE f_name = "Jan"

questions.question:
The following query produces the record set below and the sum(paid) field is incorrect –
why?
SELECT status, descrip, sum(paid) AS total
FROM member a
INNER JOIN member_item b on a.m_id = b.m_id
INNER JOIN item c on b.item_id = c.id
WHERE paid BETWEEN 33.50 and 90

GROUP BY status, descrip;
status descrip total
Retired Item3 80.80000305175781
Active Item1 67
Retired Item1 33.5
options: A. Using multiple group by columns makes math an approximation
B. Floating point math is an approximation
C. Using an alias on an aggregate field makes math an approximation
D. The number is correct, just shows too many digits

questions.question: How many foreign keys are in the member_item table?
The following query produces how many rows?\nSELECT status, descrip, sum(paid) AS total\nFROM member CROSS JOIN item
What would “CREATE INDEX master ON member (m_id)” do?
options: A. It would add an extra column to the master table with the sort order
B. It would create a small lookup table
C. It would create an additional sorted version of the master table
D. It would sort the master table in place

questions.question: The highest level of normalization met by the member table is _______.

The following query created how many rows?
SELECT f_name FROM member
UNION
SELECT f_name FROM member

What would be the result of the following query?
SELECT descrip
FROM item a
LEFT JOIN member_item b ON a.id = b.item_id
WHERE b.item_id IS NULL
options: D. Item1, Item2, Item3, Item3a, Item4, Item5, Item5a

questions.question: Which is NOT a benefit of database normalization?
options: B. Removes all null values from your data.
C. Results in a more compact database (due to less data redundancy/null values).
D. Minimizes / avoids data modification issues

questions.question: NoSQL stands for _______.
A _______ join is formed between two tables where the values of identically named and\ndefined columns are equal.
The SQL language has the ability to do conditional statements using _______.
Which of the following is NOT true about a temporary table in SQL?
options: A. The table gets deleted once the session in which it was created ends
B. Temp tables are created with SELECT .... INTO <tableName>
C. Temporary tables can be manually deleted at any time
D. You cannot alter a temporary table once its created

questions.question: Constraints can be added to a table with both a CREATE TABLE statement and an\nALTER TABLE statement
Which of the following is a table constraint in SQL?
What is a DBA when referring to SQL?
What is meant by a database trigger?
options: A. A log that a specific table was accessed
B. A log that a potentially mischievous connection was made to the database
C. A statement executed automatically when a table is modified
D. A batch process that runs at a specific time and date

questions.question: The situation where a data is stored in the database at a particular moment of time is\ncalled a(n) _______.
An advantage of a database view is it _______.
options: A. allows for easier database updates
B. reduces the dependency on pessimistic locks
D. allows for quicker updates and reads

questions.question: What can be said of the following two queries?\nSELECT fname, MAX(cost) FROM theTable\nSELECT fname, cost FROM theTable ORDER BY cost DESC LIMIT 1
options: A. The queries will return the exact same values
B. The queries may return the exact same values
C. The queries cannot return the exact same value
D. The first query will get the max, second will get the min

questions.question: A _______ table in SQL results from turning rows into columns.

What does the following query return?
SELECT member_no, name
FROM theTableAbove
WHERE name = NULL

What does the following query return?
SELECT dob
FROM theTableAbove
WHERE member_no= ‘901’
The table already has a primary key on the member_no. How would we set up a\ncomposite key on this table?
options: A. CREATE COMPOSITE INDEX myComposite ON theTableAbove (name)
B. CREATE INDEX myComposite ON theTabelAbove (name); CREATE INDEX\nmyComposite ON theTabelAbove (dob)
C. CREATE INDEX myComposite ON theTabelAbove (member_no)
D. CREATE INDEX myComposite ON theTabelAbove (name, dob)

questions.question: The theTableAbove meets the highest standardized level of normalization – 5NF.
